title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. bohemica
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. bohemica, also known as Bohemian marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Bohemian marsh orchid is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
